items.headline: The rise of a Military Junta
items.summary: The war broke the Islamic Caliphate of Shi’ism but hardened its inner core, elevating the junta within while clerical authority is a façade. U.S. and Israeli strikes have eroded Iran’s conventional and nuclear capability while empowering the IRGC, intensifyin…

items.headline: 'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
items.summary: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
items.summary: Dozens of countries sought ways to restart vital energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed more aggressive strikes on Iran, sending oil prices higher again and deepening strain on consumers. In a speech …

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.headline: HMA Moscow Nigel Casey's interview on UK-Russia relations
items.summary: British Ambassador to Moscow Nigel Casey gave an interview about the state and prospects of relations with Russia to RBC outlet on Friday 27 March 2026.

items.summary: Ukraine has kept up its attacks on Russian oil export and production infrastructure, hitting facilities in two regions, while widespread Russian drone attacks on Ukraine killed at least one civilian overnight and fighting persisted at the front, officials sai…

items.headline: US Search For Missing Crew Member Continues, As Strike Reported Near Iran Nuclear Plant
items.summary: Rescue teams are continuing to search for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, as reports emerged of a strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant.

items.headline: Russia’s Lukoil Norsi Refinery Suffers Fire From Ukrainian Hit
items.summary: A fire broke out at Lukoil PJSC’s Norsi refinery, one of the largest in Russia, following Ukraine’s latest strike on its foe’s energy infrastructure.

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

items.headline: Iran's New Supreme Leader Issues First Statement Amid Mounting Health Questions
items.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei delivered his first message as Iran's Supreme Leader through written statements read on state television, vowing to keep Strait of Hormuz closed and continue resistance. No video or audio recording has been released, raising questions about his health after the strikes that killed his father.

dimension_signal.rationale: no public appearances and health questions despite hardline stance suggest vulnerability

items.headline: IAEA Reports Latest Strike on Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Plant Violates Safety Principles
items.summary: UN nuclear chief Rafael Grossi expressed deep concern over reports of another attack on Bushehr nuclear facility, warning any strike near nuclear installations risks violating safety principles. Previous March strike damaged a structure 350 meters from reactor.

items.headline: Pakistan Army Chief Makes Nuclear Threats Against India on U.S. Soil
items.summary: General Asim Munir declared during Tampa event that Pakistan would 'take half the world down' if threatened, specifically naming India's Jamnagar refinery as potential target. India condemned remarks as nuclear sabre-rattling made on friendly nation's soil.

dimension_signal.rationale: public nuclear threats against specific civilian targets on foreign soil shows dangerous impulsiveness

items.headline: China-Pakistan Launch Five-Point Peace Plan for Iran War Ceasefire
items.summary: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar met Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Beijing to propose immediate ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and peace talks. China positions itself as mediator while maintaining economic interests in regional stability.

items.headline: Trump Approval Rating Hits New Lows Amid Iran War as Impeachment Talks Intensify
items.summary: President Trump's approval dropped to 38% in recent polls as Iran conflict escalates. House Democrats are reportedly just two votes away from impeachment over war powers violations, with critics arguing strikes lacked congressional authorization.

dimension_signal.rationale: declining approval and impeachment threats indicate reduced political accountability

items.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei Elected Iran's Third Supreme Leader After Father's Assassination
items.summary: Assembly of Experts under IRGC pressure elected Ali Khamenei's son as Supreme Leader on March 8. The 56-year-old survived US-Israeli strike that killed his father and family members, marking first hereditary succession in Islamic Republic history.

items.headline: India-Pakistan Ceasefire Agreement Violated Hours After LoC Understanding
items.summary: Pakistan brazenly violated ceasefire understanding along Line of Control and international border on same night it was reached between Directors General of Military Operations. Foreign Secretary confirmed repeated violations occurred immediately.

items.headline: Senate Rejects War Powers Resolution to Constrain Trump on Iran by 47-53 Vote
items.summary: Senate failed to pass resolution requiring congressional approval for further Iran military action. House expected similar outcome, representing latest example of Congress becoming bystander rather than decider on military operations.

items.headline: ICC Maintains Netanyahu and Gallant Arrest Warrants After Rejecting Israeli Appeals
items.summary: International Criminal Court rejected Israeli challenges to jurisdiction and upheld arrest warrants for Prime Minister Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Gallant over alleged Gaza war crimes. Israeli officials called decision antisemitic.

items.headline: IAEA Confirms Damage to Iran's Natanz Nuclear Facility Entrance Buildings
items.summary: International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed damage to entrance buildings of underground fuel enrichment plant at Natanz but no radiological consequences expected. Agency maintains emergency monitoring amid ongoing US-Israeli attacks.
